assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements prepared under IFRS.
The exposure is foreign currency risk arising from an intragroup dividend receivable that has been recognised.
The entity is considering applying IFRS 9 hedge accounting to that exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for hedging exposure to changes in fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for hedging exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposure.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context also supports ordinary IFRS accounting without hedge accounting, under which gains and losses on derivatives and on financial assets or liabilities are recognised under the normal IFRS 9 presentation and measurement requirements.
